subject: Economics
overall_learning_objectives: Critically analyze the Human Capital Index (HCI) and its impact on GDP growth
Deconstruct the 'Demographic Dividend' and the risks of 'Economic Overload'
Evaluate the structural imbalances in the Indian labor market (Educated vs. Disguised Unemployment)
Analyze the gendered dimensions of labor and National Income accounting
Assess the efficiency of the Primary, Secondary, and Tertiary sectors in labor absorption
Formulate policy recommendations for sustainable human resource development

lesson_plan.title: The Macroeconomics of Human Capital
topics_covered: Human Capital as a superior factor: Analytical comparison with Physical and Natural Capital
Case Study: Japan's post-war economic miracle through human resource investment
Theoretical framework: How education translates into 'National Income'
teaching_strategy: Socratic Seminar on 'People as a Liability vs. Asset'
Comparative data analysis of resource-poor vs. resource-rich nations
student_activities: Draft a thesis on the correlation between HCI and long-term GDP
Analyze the 'Return on Investment' in the Sakal case study
assessment: Critical essay on 'The Superiority of Human Resource'

lesson_plan.title: Sociology of Labor: Gender and GDP
topics_covered: The paradox of 'Market' vs. 'Non-Market' activities
Exclusion of household labor from National Income Accounting
Determinants of earnings: The skill-education-gender intersection
teaching_strategy: Debate on 'Accounting for Women's Domestic Labour in GDP'
Lecture on the 'Gender Pay Gap' in the unorganized sector
student_activities: Interview-based case study on the daily labor of a housewife vs. a female professional
Graphing the wage disparity across different skill levels
assessment: Analytical report on gendered labor market distortions

lesson_plan.title: Infrastructure Audit: Education Policy
topics_covered: Critical review of the 12th Five Year Plan goals
Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) and the 'Quality vs. Quantity' debate in schooling
The role of IT and Vocationalization in human capital formation
teaching_strategy: Statistical deep-dive into Literacy Rate disparities (SC/ST, Gender, Rural/Urban)
Policy audit of 'Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan' and 'Bridge Courses'
student_activities: Interpret complex multi-variable tables on education expenditure
Design a 'Vocational Training Module' for a rural secondary school

lesson_plan.title: The Health-Productivity Nexus
topics_covered: Life Expectancy and IMR as proxies for economic productivity
Health infrastructure as a prerequisite for Human Capital
National Health Policy: Reaching the 'last mile' population
teaching_strategy: Lecture on 'Economic Overload' caused by high morbidity
Analysis of the health-workforce attendance correlation
student_activities: Calculate the change in 'Dependency Ratio' based on life expectancy trends
Create a 'Health Resource Map' for an underserved district
assessment: Short answer test on health indicators

lesson_plan.title: Labor Market Failures: Disguised Unemployment
topics_covered: Mathematical modeling of 'Marginal Productivity' in the Primary sector
The 'Invisible' nature of rural unemployment
Impact of land fragmentation on labor efficiency
teaching_strategy: Numerical simulation: Calculating 'Zero Marginal Productivity' in a family farm
student_activities: Solve a complex problem set on labor surplus in agriculture

lesson_plan.title: Urban Paradox: Educated Unemployment
topics_covered: The 'Paradoxical Manpower Situation': Surplus vs. Shortage
Why matriculates have higher employment rates than graduates
Psychological and social consequences of long-term unemployment
teaching_strategy: Lecture on the 'Skill Gap' in the Secondary and Tertiary sectors
Group brainstorming on the 'Wastage of Manpower Resource'
student_activities: Draft a policy brief to reduce 'Educated Unemployment' in Tier-2 cities
Analyze a case study on the migration of rural labor to the Tertiary sector

lesson_plan.title: Sectoral Absorption & Final Synthesis
topics_covered: The shift from Primary to Secondary/Tertiary sectors in India
The role of 'New Services' (IT, Biotechnology) in labor absorption
Comprehensive chapter review
teaching_strategy: Final mind-mapping session: 'The Future of Indian Human Capital'
student_activities: Complete a sectoral transition chart showing labor shifts
Participate in a whole-chapter 'Grand Revision' seminar
assessment: Comprehensive unit test (30 marks) featuring case-study based questions

teaching_aids: Complex data sets on IMR, GER, and Literacy
Case study handouts on Japan and India’s IT sector
evaluation_methods: Advanced Data Interpretation and Numerical Solving
teacher_tip: Push students to see the 'Demographic Dividend' as a time-bound window; if we do not invest in health and education *now*, the bulge in the working-age population will become a social liability rather than an economic asset.
